observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) create an Inventory Committee, who will be temporarily relieved of all their regular
duties, to complete the physical inventory in three (3) months or less;
b) supervise, thru the Committee, the preparation of the Physical Inventory Plan and
the submission of a copy to the COA Audit Team, at least ten (10) calendar days
before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities;
c) supervise the conduct of the physical count and the immediate preparation of a
Report on Physical Count of Property using the prescribed form and the submission
of the report to the Audit Team; and,
d) initiate the implementation of all prior years’ audit recommendations on PPE
account, as well as, the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE
accounts” as provided in the COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to
correct the Infrastructure Assets account balances and the corresponding
accumulated depreciation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary
adjusting entries to correct the balance of the Trust Liability-DRRM account.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the unexpended
balances from CY 2020 and 2021 amounting to ₱20,885.70 and ₱1,699,425.05,
respectively, to the separate Trust Fund-MDRRMF bank account.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer, through the
LDRRM Council, include in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, under separate
caption, the list of PPAs charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous
years.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct as soon as possible a
physical count of inventories and cause the preparation and submission of the
Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Municipal Accountant
on or before June 30, 2022.

We also recommended that:
a) The Property Officer summarize all the Requisition and Issue Slips
received during the year in a Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
and submit to the Municipal Accountant for the recognition in the books
of the cost of the consumed inventories; and,
b) The Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the issued/distributed/
consumed inventories based on the report of the Property Officer and
reconcile the balance with the RPCI.

Moving forward, we recommended that Property Officer record all purchases
in Stock Cards; issue inventories based on approved RIS; consolidate all the
RIS in the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the same to
the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th day after the end of every month.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit
an explanation on the non-conduct of reconciliation of their accounts periodically to
detect errors and discrepancies and correct these immediately.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the unrecorded
transactions and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
misstatements noted in audit.

We recommended that the Chairman on Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
require the winning bidders or contractors to indicate the date of receipt on
the Notice of Award and Notice to Proceed on the space provided, to determine
the timelines and deadlines of each procurement activity, as well as to ensure
that terms and conditions of the contract will have force and effect.

We recommended that the Chairperson of the Bid and Awards Committee
(BAC) submit to the Audit Team justification on the non- observance of the
required period of each procurement activities, including the submission of
the approved contract duly supported with all required documents within the
reglementary period. This is without prejudice to the issuance of a Notice of
Suspension on defective transactions, if warranted.

Henceforth, the Chairperson of the BAC shall ensure that the LGU abides by
the standards set forth by RA 9184 and its Implementing Rules and
Regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a) submit justification for the payment made on Disbursement Vouchers
which were not supported with complete documents and for the payments
made before the inspection was conducted; and,
b) henceforth, ensure that all Disbursement Vouchers were duly supported
with complete documents.

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
a) submit justification for the deficiencies noted in the preparation of
Statement of Work Accomplished, Inspection Report, Certificate of
Project Completion and Certificate of Project Acceptance.
b) Henceforth, ensure that all documentary requirements for progress billing,
final payment and release of retention money are properly prepared.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor, as Chairman of the Municipal
Development Council, and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer,
identify and include in the AIP priority projects which are ready for
implementation in the current budget year to ensure optimum utilization of
the 20% DF.

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
a) Conduct a detailed engineering of all projects prior to its inclusion in the
AIP which includes the preparation of the Program of Work with the
Detailed Estimates and plans and specifications;
b) Submit to the Bids and Awards Committee the Program of Work with the
Detailed Estimates, plans/specifications and Purchase Request within the
timetable indicated in the AIP, taking into consideration the period within
which the procurement process and project implementation will be
completed; and
c) Fast-track the implementation of priority projects identified for CY 2022
and in the subsequent years;

We further recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee schedule the
procurement of projects in accordance with the timeframe specified in the AIP
to ensure completion of the projects before the end of the year.

We recommended that BAC Chairman and the MDRRM Officer:
a) submit a written explanation on the noted deficiencies in the procurement
of Early Warning Sign; and
b) submit to the Municipal Accountant the lacking supporting documents to
the Audit Team for review and evaluation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant together with the
Municipal Treasurer submit explanation why payment for the lot was made
despite absence of complete supporting documents.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor supervise the strict
observance and compliance on the procurement activities mandated under the
Government Procurement Reform Act or R.A No. 9184 and its Implementing
Rules and Regulations; and the submission of all required supporting
documents to establish its propriety and validity.

We recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer in coordination with
Municipal Accountant, prepare and submit the monthly Report on Sources
and Utilization of Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month,
through the LDRRMC and LDC, to the COA Auditor.

AAR CY 2019, Part II, No. 4 | No separate books of accounts, inventory of facilities and bank account was maintained for the operation of the water system with a carrying value of ₽22,114,943.48 under Water Supply System account in the books of the Municipality of Culaba, contrary to Sec. 313, Article II,  RA 7160, such that the viability of its operations, maintenance cost and available funds and properties could not be determined, monitored and validated. | We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate books of accounts for the Municipality’s water system operations.  

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, with the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Mayor, open a separate bank account for its water system. 

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the Municipal Engineer requiring the said official to conduct of physical inventory in CY 2020 and render a report upon completion, on the water system structures and facilities turned-over to Culaba Water District and require its submission to COA and the Municipal Accountant for the latter’s appropriate adjustment in the accounting records.

AAR CY 2019, Part II, No. 9 | The Municipal Treasurer have not reported the missing Accountable Form 51 (ORs) with Serial Numbers 1859145-150 since November 7, 2017,   since the Consolidated Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) were not supported with Collectors’ Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and were not thoroughly checked, contrary to Sections 50 and 51 of NGAS Manual, Volume II, resulting in possible misuse of the said unaccounted accountable forms | We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Treasurer and Mr. Lucahin to present the above missing accountable forms and remit the equivalent amount collected therefrom. Also, require collectors to prepare the RAAF monthly for consolidation in the CRAAF by the Municipal Treasurer and submit the same to COA monthly.  

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer record all received and issued accountable forms and ensure safekeeping of the unissued AF.

AAR CY 2017, Part II, No. 3 | 19.Rice allowance totaling P1,203,600.00 was granted to officials and employees without any legal basis, in violation of Section 12 of RA No. 6758 and Chapters II and III of COA Circulars 2013-003 thereby resulting in illegal disbursements. | 66) We recommend that the Municipal Mayor initiate the refund of the amount from all persons liable thereto.

AAR CY 2017, Part II, No 10 | 22. The Local School Board did not appropriate in the SEF budget funds for the Early Childhood Care Development (ECCD) program and appropriated instead for expenditures which were not authorized to be funded therefrom, contrary to RA No 10410 and DEPED-DBM-DILG JMC No 1 s, 2017, thus, priority programs and activities for children 4 years and below was not undertaken while funds for the improvement of schools were diverted. | 70) We recommended that the LCE and all the members of the Local School Board ensure that programs and activities for ECCD are incorporated in the budget for SEF.

AAR CY 2015, Part II, No. 9 | 26. The Bids and Award Committee (BAC) failed to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) at the end of the 1st and 2nd semesters of calendar year 2015 and eventually failed to submit the same to the Government Procurement Policy Board. | 76) We recommended that the BAC should prepare the PMR and submit the approved PMR to the GPPB in the prescribed format as required by law.

 77) Also, we recommended that the municipal Mayor ensures the preparation and submission of the required report.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Mayor compel the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of all LGU properties as soon as possible, mark counted assets with property tags and submit the required Reports on Physical Count thereon with complete details to the Audit Team not later than April 30, 2021.

We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer, Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant shall coordinate each other in providing information on the cost or fair market value for each listed road in the Report on the Physical Count of Road Network as well as in the reconciliation of its records.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian should maintain Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for all property of the LGU and reconcile these with the Reports on the Physical Count of PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should:
maintain complete and accurate records of PPE Ledger Cards of which year-end balances should tally with the amounts reported in the Statement of Financial Position; and
compute the correct depreciation of all properties under PPE, except Land, every year and submit the computation schedule of the annual depreciation of PPEs to the Audit Team every January of each year.

The Municipal Accountant during the exit conference agreed to evaluate the records and make necessary adjustments to correct the cited errors.

We recommended that the Property Officer should conduct a physical count of unissued inventories as of April 15, 2021 and should prepare a Report on Physical Count of Inventories.  This shall be submitted to the Municipal Accountant for recording the undistributed inventories in the books of accounts.

Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should record procurement of goods in accordance with the provisions of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.

The Municipal Treasurer should submit to the Municipal Accountant all the disbursement vouchers pertaining to the checks issued for transactions CY 2020 for recording by the Municipal Accountant.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant should record these vouchers in the books and submit these to the Audit Team immediately, otherwise, the Audit Team will issue a Notice of Suspension based on the Bank Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should prepare adjusting entries   to correct the misstatements noted in audit and record the accurate amounts in the ledgers. She should review thoroughly the collections and deposits before recording in the books and reconcile her records with that of the Municipal Treasurer every month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Social Worker to submit the distribution list duly signed by the actual recipients/beneficiaries and any evidence to support the actual distribution of rice.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and members of the Development Council shall:
identify and include in the AIP projects which are ready for implementation in the current budget year to ensure its completion; and
request the assistance of all concerned officials in the preparation of the budget particularly on cost estimates of PPAs in order to arrive at a sufficient amount of appropriation.

The recommendations of the Audit Team are noted and will be taken-up.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Officer and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking documents to support the payment for meals and snack amounting ₱368,300.00 and written justification why the ₱200,850.00 for the conduct of formulation of risk informed CDP shall not be disallowed in audit.

Henceforth, stop conducting activities on expensive venues where same could have been done at office premises with lesser cost. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension/Disallowance, if warranted.

We recommended that the PESO Manager should submit immediately to the Audit Team the copies of the MOA, approved DOLE work program and the contract of service of the TUPAD workers for validation of transactions.

The PESO Manager was able to submit the requested documents, however the Audit Team emphasized during the exit conference meeting that the complete supporting documents of transactions should be submitted on time to the Municipal Accountant to avoid delays not only in the recording and submission of the transaction documents but as well as to the review and evaluation of such transaction of the Audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Worker adhere to the documentary requirements pursuant to DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 2020-009 and should submit all the lacking documents to support the validity of the claims.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary documentary requirements required under COA Circular 2012-001 including the proof that conditions cited under DBM Budget Circular No. 2020-5 were complied with.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should:
deposit all collections daily and intact;
stop the practice of encashment of checks utilizing the daily collections; and
coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the issuance of Automatic Debit Advice (ADA) for any fund transfer within the accounts of the LGU or otherwise issue a corresponding Official Receipt under the receiving fund.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant should record or make adjustment under GF account of the unrecorded fund transfer totaling ₱14,001.11.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should submit all the reports due for submission for the months of January and February, 2021.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor should take appropriate action to instill discipline to all concerned officials, on the submission of all financial and operational documents or reports, within the timeline prescribed by laws and regulations.  Impose sanctions where necessary to improve the submission of financial reports and accounts of the Municipality of Culaba.

observations.managementAction:
Conducted physical count only on Local Road Networks, Public Infrastructures, Buildings and Other Structures.
To prepare Inventory Reports of other PPEs 



















No actions taken.

 







No actions taken yet.

observations.recommendation:
AAR CY 2019,
Part II, No. 2 | Purchase of generators amounting to P1,053,915.00 and goods amounting to P191,181.00 out of the LDRRMF for distribution were erroneously recorded as outright expenses of the LGU instead of recording the same initially as equipment and inventories, contrary to IPSAS 17 and 12, thus the affected accounts were misstated and accountability of the goods were no longer accounted upon distribution. | We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record supplies and materials as Inventories using the correct account codes prescribed in the New Chart of Accounts and recognize expenses only when duly supported with distribution reports.

AAR CY 2019,
Part II, No. 3 | Goods for distribution totaling P1,581,709.00 paid from funds downloaded by the National Government were recorded directly as expenses upon purchase instead of recognizing the items as Inventories, contrary to IPSAS 12, thus, assets were understated, undistributed items were no longer accounted as inventories while income on subsidy was overstated. | We recommended that the Municipal Accountant: 

Record goods as Inventories and equipment as PPEs upon purchase using the correct account codes regardless of source of fund; 

Record the expense only when supported with Distribution List and Summary of Supplies and Material Issued (RSSMI) or Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment from the Property Officer and program focal persons; and

Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers (SLs) for each fund transfer.

AAR CY 2019, Part II, No. 5 | The budget allotted for loan amortization payments was not monitored such that payments in CY 2019 exceeded its appropriation by ₽588,772.42, contrary to Sections 2 and 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, resulting in irregular disbursements.  Also, the Municipal Accountant did not reclassify to Current Liabilities the principal amount of the loan at ₽2,685,838.32 which was due and demandable in CY 2020, contrary to IPSAS 1, thus presentation of liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position was misleading. | We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer monitor the appropriations available for loan amortization payments to avoid misuse of funds.


We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant obtain from the bank the Debit/Credit Memos and support the same in the Journal Entry Voucher to record the transactions.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other officials concerned refund the amount of ₽588,772.42 paid to MDFO without an appropriation.

AAR CY 2019, Part II, No. 6 | Completed structures and project totaling P5,160,617.60 under the Yolanda Rehabilitation and Recovery Program (YRRP) funded by the Department of Agriculture and by other national government agencies remain idle and unutilized, contrary to the provisions in the Memorandum of Agreement, thus, the intended beneficiaries were deprived of the immediate benefits of the projects. | We recommended that the Municipal Mayor expedite the distribution of completed of structures under the YRRP and other programs of the national government to the intended beneficiaries in order to provide them the expected benefits.
  
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide funds and procure the facilities needed for the National Child Development Center, after the designated official had identified the items needed for its operation

AAR CY 2019, Part II, No. 7 | Goods totaling ₽2,213,339.00 were paid even if no deliveries were made, contrary to Section 88 of PD 1445, since the Inspection and Acceptance Reports stated that deliveries were completed, while most disbursements were not supported with complete documents, contrary to Section 88 of PD 1445, thereby resulting in irregular expenditures and doubtful validity of transactions. | We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 



prohibit the Property Officer from receiving checks of suppliers and to submit his explanations for doing such acts together with the Municipal Treasurer for releasing checks to persons other than the payees; and

obtain written explanations from the signatories of the Inspection and Acceptance Report for the untruthful statements on completeness of deliveries, and warn the employees’ concerned against repeating similar acts in the future. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant process DVs for payments only when supported with complete supporting documents.

AAR CY 2019,
Part II, No 10 and AAR CY 2018, Part II, No. 2 | 10. Discrepancies occurred in the monthly balances between the cashbooks of the Municipal Treasurer and subsidiary ledgers of the Municipal Accountant on Cash in Local Treasury and Advances accounts due to the failure of both officials to reconcile their respective records at least quarterly, contrary to Item 6.3 of COA Circular No. No. 97-002, hence, errors in recording were not immediately detected and corrected. | We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer: 

record accurately all transactions in the cashbook, compute the monthly totals and record as beginning balances for the next ensuing month the ending balances of previous month; 


 review thoroughly the recorded collections and deposits in the cashbook to avoid errors and provide accurate balances;

 reconcile cashbook entries with records of the Municipal Accountant at the end of each month at least once every quarter; and 


submit all transactions to the Municipal Accountant on or before the 5th day of each succeeding month so the latter could record the same in the books immediately.


Trace previous records to identify the variances in the cashbooks and general ledgers.


We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the misstatements noted in audit and record accurate amounts in the SL and GL.

AAR CY 2019, Part II, No. 11 | 11. Collections were not remitted daily and in full by collectors to the Municipal Treasurer while deposits of collections exposed the funds to the risk of loss through misuse or misappropriation while in the hands of accountable officers, contrary to Section 33 of the Revised Cash Examination Manual and other existing rules and regulations. | We recommend that the Municipal Mayor compel the collectors to remit daily ALL their collections to the Municipal Treasurer and for the latter to deposit the FULL amount of his collections to LBP to protect the funds of the LGU.  Also, record all transactions in the cashbook on the date of actual deposit.

AAR CY 2019,
Part II, No. 12 | 12. Taxes withheld from employees, suppliers and contractors monthly were not fully remitted to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR), contrary to Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2-12 and Revenue Regulations No. 2-98, thus, the funds could be utilized for other purposes and LGU personnel may be held liable for charges and penalties that may be imposed by the said agency. | We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer remit the full amount of taxes withheld within ten (10) days after the end of each month to avoid penalties and surcharges.

AAR CY 2019,
Part II, No.13 | 13. Monthly contributions to GSIS withheld from salaries of some employees were not remitted, contrary to Section 6 (b) of GSIS Act No. 8291, thus the employees concerned may not be able to avail of the benefits due them. | We recommended that the Municipal Accountant remit to the GSIS the full amount withheld from employees’ salaries to avoid incurrence of surcharges and enable the employees to avail the benefits which they are entitled to.

AAR CY 2019,
Part II, No. 15 | 15. No funds were allocated for the implementation of Solid Waste Management (SWM) for CY 2019 and the Municipal Waste Management Board was not yet created by the LGU, contrary to Republic Act No 9003, thus the targeted activities during the year were not implemented and efficient management of solid wastes generated within its jurisdiction were not fully attained. | 59) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and submit the same to the Sangguniang Bayan for the issuance of a Resolution approving such Board.

60) We also recommended that the designated SWM Focal Person request the Budget Officer and the Municipal Mayor to appropriate funds in its annual budget, for the Solid Waste Management Program of the municipality which may be from the 20% Local Development Fund or collection of fees for provision of solid waste services.

AAR CY 2019,
Part II, No. 16 | 16. The GAD Accomplishment Report as of December 31, 2019 reported programs which were not yet fully accomplished in CY 2019 such that the inadequate cost of actual implementation on GAD-related activities was not disclosed, as required in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2016-01. | 61) We recommended that the GAD Focal Point prepare an accurate GAD Accomplishment Report and ensure that all programs and activities in the DILG-Approved GAD Plan and Budget are fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had accomp-lished their targetted programs except those activities that were not realized due to pandemic.

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2018, Part II, No. 3 | 17. The delayed implementation of projects in the AIP led the LGU to complete only three out of six projects utilizing P6,236,442.92 only or 66% of the P11,956,058.20 appropriations of the 20% Development Fund, contrary to DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1, thus socio-economic and environmental benefits were not fully provided to the residents of the Municipality. | 62) We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer Implement all projects in the AIP during the year to avoid the stagnation of the municipality’s development.

AAR CY 2017, Part II 7 | 20. Copies of Approved Contracts, Purchase Orders and supporting documents in the procurement of infrastructure projects and goods and services with aggregate gross amount of P65,333,904.59 and P5,358,279.31, respectively were not submitted promptly to COA, contrary to COA Circular No. 2009-001, thereby precluding the immediate verification on the validity of the transactions, inspection of the items procured and transmittal to management of audit results. | 67) We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and the designated Supply Officer to submit consistently to the Audit Team, within 5 days upon perfection, the Approved Contracts and Purchase Orders, together with the required supporting documents to preclude audit suspension/ disallowance of the transaction.

68) We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant process payment to contractors and suppliers only when the same is supported with the transmittal Letter of the BAC Secretariat that the complete procurement documents were already submitted and duly received.

AAR CY 2017, Part II, No 9 | 21. The LGU was not able to fully implement the targeted plans for its Solid Waste Management Program in CY 2017, including the construction of Materials Recovery Facilities and procurement of garbage truck despite the available appropriations of P1,261,220.77, thus, efficient and effective management of solid wastes generated within its jurisdiction was not fully attained, contrary to Sections 10 and 12, Item 2 of RA No. 9003. | 69) We recommended that the LCE ensure that the SWM Focal Person implement fully the identified Plans, Programs and Activities in the SWMAP for an efficient utilization of the budget for the said program.
observations.managementAction: The Muni-cipality is partially imple-menting the SMWAP under the super-vision of the SWM Focal Person and LCE

observations.recommendation: AAR CY 2016, Part II, No. 5 | 25. Activities which were not capital expenditures amounting to P90,000.00 were appropriated from the 20% Development Fund contrary to Item 4.0 of DILG-DBM JMC No 2011-1, thus, funds for development projects were reduced and limited. Likewise, subsidiary ledgers were not maintained to monitor the 20% DF and its utilization, contrary to Section 111 of PD 1445, thus the unutilized amount cannot be easily determined and any given time. | 75) We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for the 20% development fund to account for all disbursements and balances of the fund and reconcile immediately the balances from the records of the MBO.

We reiterate our previous recommendations that the Municipal Mayor
constitute immediately an Inventory Committee who shall perform and
complete the following tasks before the end of CY 2020:

1.26.1 conduct physical count of LGU properties and mark counted assets
with Property Tags;
1.26.2 prepare the RPCPPE with complete details;
1.26.3 submit the RPCPPE to the Municipal Accountant who shall reconcile
the same with her records, and;
1.26.4 locate the missing properties of the LGU and submit a report thereon
for appropriate legal action on the persons responsible for its loss.

We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant:

1.27.1 Coordinate with the Property Officer and the Municipal Engineer to
identify and prepare the list of assets under the Water Supply System
account which were transferred to Culaba Water District, confirm the
nature of such transfer and disclose the same in the Notes to FS;
1.27.2 Record all procurement as well as payments of on-going and completed
infrastructure projects in the appropriate accounts as prescribed in the
PPSAS Revised Chart of Accounts;
1.27.3 Record the tractor donated to the LGU by DA RFO8 in the Agricultural
and Forestry Equipment account;
1.27.4 Deduct at all times the required 10% Retention money on every
progress billing of contractors and record the amount deducted in the
Construction In-Progress account with corresponding entry to
Guaranty/Security Deposit Payable account;
1.27.5 Record all road networks projects in the books pertaining to projects
prior to CY 2015 and derecognize the carrying value of projects which
has undergone re-blocking or repairs;
1.27.6 Prepare the accounting entry to transfer from the books of the
Municipality the cost of completed barangay projects to the books of
the recipient Barangay;
1.27.7 Review and sign each JEV prepared by the accounting staff;
1.27.8 Maintain complete General Ledgers, Subsidiary Ledgers, PPE Ledger
Cards, Real Property Ledger Cards; and
1.27.9 Prepare the complete and accurate data in the Financial Statements
per Fund to support the consolidated FS.

We also recommend that the Designated Property Officer prepare and
maintain the following:

1.28.1 Property Cards for all assets of the LGU based on the RPCPPE and
every time there are new procured assets;
1.28.2 Issue the Inventory Custodian Slips (ICS) for supplies issued and the
Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) for equipment issued to
all employees who are custodians of LGU assets; and
1.28.3 Renew PAR every three years to validate the persons accountable.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant record supplies and materials
as Inventories using the correct account codes prescribed in the New Chart of
Accounts and recognize expenses only when duly supported with distribution
reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

3.7.1 Record goods as Inventories and equipment as PPEs upon purchase
using the correct account codes regardless of source of fund;
3.7.2 Record the expense only when supported with Distribution List and
Summary of Supplies and Material Issued or Acknowledgement
Receipt for Equipment from the Property Officer and program focal
persons; and
3.7.3 Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for each fund transfer.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate books of
accounts for the Municipality’s water system operations.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, with the approval of the
Sangguniang Bayan and the Municipal Mayor, open a separate bank account
for its water system.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue a directive to the
Municipal Engineer requiring the said official to conduct of physical inventory
in CY 2020 and render a report upon completion, on the water system
structures and facilities turned-over to Culaba Water District and require its
submission to COA and the Municipal Accountant for the latter’s appropriate
adjustment in the accounting records.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer monitor the
appropriations available for loan amortization payments to avoid misuse of
funds.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant obtain from the bank
the Debit/Credit Memos and support the same in the Journal Entry Voucher
to record the transactions.

Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other officials
concerned refund the amount of ₽588,772.42 paid to MDFO without an
appropriations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor expedite the distribution of
completed of structures under the YRRP and other programs of the national
government to the intended beneficiaries in order to provide them the
expected benefits.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide funds and procure
the facilities needed for the National Child Development Center, after the
designated official had identified the items needed for its operation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
7.9.1 prohibit the Property Officer from receiving checks of suppliers and to
submit his explanations for doing such acts together with the Municipal
Treasurer for releasing checks to persons other than the payees; and
7.9.2 obtain written explanations from the signatories of the Inspection and
Acceptance Report for the untruthful statements on completeness of
deliveries, and warn the employees’ concerned against repeating
similar acts in the future.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant process DVs for
payments only when supported with complete supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
8.11.1. Constitute a Local Finance Committee who shall review the
Municipal Revenue Code of 2007 and prepare a list of revisions
adopting an updated and realistic rates to be collected from its
revenue-generating properties;
8.11.2. Submit to the Sangguniang Bayan immediately the proposed
revisions on the Municipal Revenue Code for the issuance of the
appropriate Ordinance;
8.11.3. Renew all contracts with market stallholders including other areas
for rent and revise the Contract of Lease Agreements to incorporate
the latest revised approved rates and delete provisions which are
unfavorable to the LGU.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
8.12.1. conduct an inspection and inventory of all stalls for rent by the
Municipality;
8.12.2. prepare a list thereof with the names of the lessees of each stall
8.12.3. submit said list to the Municipal Mayor as input in the renewal of
Contracts;
8.12.4. enforce payment of the unpaid rent from CY 2018 to CY 2019.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Treasurer
and Mr. Lucahin to present the above missing accountable forms and remit the
equivalent amount collected therefrom. Also, require collectors to prepare the
RAAF monthly for consolidation in the CRAAF by the Municipal Treasurer and
submit the same to COA monthly.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer record all received and
issued accountable forms and ensure safekeeping of the unissued AF.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer: (1) record accurately all
transactions in the cashbook, compute the monthly totals and record as
beginning balances for the next ensuing month the ending balances of previous
month; (2) review thoroughly the recorded collections and deposits in the
cashbook to avoid errors and provide accurate balances; reconcile
cashbook entries with records of the Municipal Accountant at the end of each month at least once every quarter; and (4) submit all transactions to the Municipal
Accountant on or before the 5th day of each succeeding month so the latter
could record the same in the books immediately.

We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries
to correct the misstatements noted in audit and record accurate amounts in
the SL and GL.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor compel the collectors to remit daily
ALL their collections to the Municipal Treasurer and for the latter to deposit
the FULL amount of his collections to LBP to protect the funds of the LGU.
Also, record all transactions in the cashbook on the date of actual deposit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer remit the full amount of taxes withheld within ten (10) days after the
end of each month to avoid penalties and surcharges.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant remit to the GSIS the full
amount withheld from employees’ salaries to avoid incurrence of surcharges
and enable the employees to avail the benefits which they are entitled to.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

14.11.1 pay the mandated daily minimum wage prescribed by the RTWPB
for Region VIII;
14.11.2 reduce the number of Job Order personnel in the LGU to those
which are considered essential and urgent;
14.11.3 revise the Contract of Service of Job Order personnel to include
information on their specific duties, assigned areas, rates, period
covered by the contract, address, with pictures and signed as
acknowledgement by the JO being hired;
14.11.4 require all JOs, prior to payment of wages, to submit an
accomplishment report, duly filled-up and validated by the LGU
official who is the JO’s immediate supervisory; and
14.11.5 compel the Accountant and Treasurer to pay JO wages only when
the payroll is duly supported with Contracts (initial payment) and
Accomplishment Reports signed by Heads of Offices where they are
assigned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor constitute the Municipal Solid
Waste Management Board and submit the same to the Sangguniang Bayan
for the issuance of a Resolution approving such Board.

We also recommended that the designated SWM Focal Person request the
Budget Officer and the Municipal Mayor to appropriate funds in its annual
budget, for the Solid Waste Management Program of the municipality which
may be from the 20% Local Development Fund or collection of fees for
provision of solid waste services.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point prepare an accurate GAD
Accomplishment Report and ensure that all programs and activities in the
DILG-Approved GAD Plan and Budget are fully implemented.

We recommended that the LCE and all the members of the Local School Board ensure that programs and activities for ECCD are incorporated in the budget for SEF. 

Henceforth, discontinue the use of SEF for expenses which are not authorized to be funded therefrom.
observations.managementAction: The LGU no longer included items not in line with the utilization of the SEF fund

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the BAC should prepare the PMR and submit the approved PMR to the GPPB in the prescribed format as required by law.

Also, we recommended that the municipal Mayor ensures the preparation and submission of the required report.

1.9         We recommended that:

      1.9.1       The Municipal Accountant:
           (a) record all donated assets, including the  donated  ambulance
              van based on  the  fair  market  value,  if  no  document  is
              available as to its cost or obtain documents from  the  donor
              agency to obtain its acquisition cost;
           (b)  maintain the Property Ledger Cards; and
           (c)  reconcile accounting records with the RCPPE once  submitted
              and with the records of the designated Property Officer


      1.9.2             The  Municipal  Assessor  obtain  and  complete  the
           documentary requirements for the transfer of ownership  of  lots
           purchased by, or donated to, the LGU.


      1.9.3       The Designated Property Officer:


          a) Provide Property Tags for each equipment with complete labels;
          b) Update the ARE of each employee every three years;
          c) Provide adequate storage for unserviceable equipment;
          d) Request for disposal of unserviceable properties;
          e)  Maintain  Property  Cards   for   all   properties   of   the
             Municipality.


      1.9.4            The Municipal Mayor constitute an Inventory Committee
           who shall conduct a physical count of LGU properties in CY 2019,
           mark counted assets with Property Tags and  prepare  the  RPCPPE
           with complete details for submission to the Municipal Accountant
           who shall reconcile the same with her records.

9. We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the  Municipal
      Treasurer: (1) trace previous records to identify the variances in the
      cashbooks and general ledgers;  and  (2)  reconcile  their  respective
      balances monthly.  Also, we recommended that the Municipal  Accountant
      draw possible adjusting entries to correct the errors noted.

11. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairman of the  Local
      Development Council, exclude in the AIP, programs and activities which
      are NOT capital or investment in nature so that  the  20%  Development
      Fund  will  be  appropriated  and  utilized   solely   for   long-term
      infrastructure projects.

  12. We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement all projects
      in  the  AIP  during  the  year  to  avoid  the  stagnation   of   the
      municipality’s development.

4.5.  We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:


     1. Maintain and update subsidiary  ledgers  (SL)  for  LDRRMF  in  the
        General Fund, with separate SL for 30% QRF and for the 70% MF;


     2. Maintain and update subsidiary ledgers for the LDRRMF Trust Fund by
        year of transfer or by donor, if any;


     3. Coordinate with the Budget  Officer  and  the  LDRRMO  monthly  and
        reconcile respective balances to arrive at the  correct  amount  of
        the unutilized LDRRMF.


     4. Submit, with the assistance of the MDRRMO, an  accurate  Report  on
        Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF, on or before the 15th day of the
        end of each month, to the COA Audit Team.


4.6.  We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer,  with  the  assistance
      of the Municipal Accountant, transfer the unutilized balances  of  the
      previous year’s LDRRMF from the General Fund to the Trust  Fund  using
      the “Trust Liability-DRRM” account to protect the  LDRRMF  from  being
      utilized for other purposes.

observations.managementAction: Inspection and recording of properties per Division already undertaken but no RPCPPE submitted; Property Officer still does not maintain Property Cards, thus, no reconciliation is being done by the accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Development Council to include only in the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan development projects with socio-economic and environmental impact as explicitly provided in DILG/DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommend further that the LCE, the Mun. Budget Officer, the Mun. Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer desist from utilizing the 20% Development Fund for administrative expenditures in order to avoid audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: There are no longer expenditures charged to the 20% Development Fund which are not capitalizable in nature.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor stop the grant of allowances and such other benefits to LGU officials and employees without legal basis. 

We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor initiate the refund of the amount from all persons liable thereto.
observations.managementAction: No Rice Allowance was granted for succeeding year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant always require the submission of ORs to support claims for taxi fares otherwise, no payments should be made for such purpose. 

Also, to review thoroughly the completeness of the supporting documents and ensure the legality, correctness and reasonableness of the amounts being claimed before payments for travelling expenses are approved.
observations.managementAction: Management required the submission of complete documents before the processing of the claims.
The LGU no longer grants new loans for employees with net take home pays below the GAA requirement but can no longer do anything about existing loans.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the lacking disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid penal sanctions. In future, submit all documents on the transactions of the LGU, trial balances and other financial reports within the timeline prescribed.

Further, we recommended that the LCE impose the prescribed penal provisions when the said officials fail to render accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and the designated Supply Officer to submit consistently to the Audit Team, within 5 days upon perfection, the Approved Contracts and Purchase Orders, together with the required supporting documents to preclude audit suspension/disallowance of the transaction.

We further recommended that each project documents be filed in an orderly manner, contained individually in an expanding envelope, properly labeled according to the Name of the Project, Project ID, Location, Contractor, Contract Amount, Source of Fund and Date of Bidding.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant process payment to contractors and suppliers only when the same is supported with the transmittal Letter of the BAC Secretariat that the complete procurement documents were already submitted and duly received.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor agreed and required the implementation of the audit recommendation.




The Perfected Contracts submitted were orderly filed and organized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials concerned to submit all require reports to the Audit Team within the prescribed timelines for prompt evaluation of the agency’s accomplishments, timely communication of results and avoid the imposition of sanctions for non-rendition of reports and accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Mayor required the various Department Heads and concerned employees to submit reports and render their accounts available to the auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE ensure that the SWM Focal Person implement fully the identified Plans, Programs and Activities in the SWMAP for an efficient utilization of the budget for the said program. 

Also, oversee the preparation of the aforementioned Plan so that the targets embodied therein are those which are actually and presently needed by the LGU and its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is partially implementing the SMWAP under the supervision of the SWM Focal Person and LCE

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LDRRMO prepare annually LDRRMFIP before the start of the year to support the amount and the activities indicated in the AIP and ensure that the disbursements are authorized by the LDRRM Council and the SB.

Also, we recommended that the LDRRMO submit the report on sources and utilization on LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC to the COA to immediately determine it the utilization is responsive to risk reduction.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP was formulated during the start of the budget year.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for the 20% development fund to account for all disbursements and balances of the fund and reconcile immediately the balances from the records of the MBO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:


     a. the Inventory Committee to confirm  through  inspection  the  listed
        properties per Division, consolidate the list using  the  RPCPPE  in
        the prescribed format duly approved by the LCE and submit  the  said
        report within the prescribed period to the offices concerned;


     b. the Property Officer to maintain Property Cards for each kind of PPE
        owned by the Agency and  reconcile  the  same  with  the  accounting
        records, and;


     c. the Municipal Accountant  to  maintain  and  update  the  prescribed
        PPELC, reconcile the same monthly with property records, compute the
        depreciation of all depreciable  PPEs  and  present  accurately  all
        accounts in the Financial Statements in accordance with the  Revised
        Chart of Accounts for LGUs.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  the  Municipal
Development Council to include only in  the  LGU’s  Annual  Investment  Plan
development  projects  with  socio-economic  and  environmental  impact   as
explicitly provided in DILG/DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01.


      We recommended further that the Local Chief Executive,  the  Municipal
Budget  Officer,  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the  Municipal  Treasurer
desist  from  utilizing  the  20%  Development   Fund   for   administrative
expenditures in order to avoid audit disallowances.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor stop the grant of allowances and
such other benefits to LGU officials  and  employees  without  legal  basis.
We also recommend that the  Municipal  Mayor  initiate  the  refund  of  the
amount from all persons liable thereto.

We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  always  require   the
submission of ORs to support claims for taxi fares  otherwise,  no  payments
should  be  made  for  such  purpose.  Also,  to   review   thoroughly   the
completeness  of  the  supporting  documents  and   ensure   the   legality,
correctness and reasonableness of the amounts being claimed before  payments
for travelling expense are approved.


      We also recommend that the Local Chief Executive require all employees
to exercise prudence when filing claims for  official  travels  and  observe
strictly  the  existing  provisions  of  the  afore-cited  laws,  rules  and
regulations.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  direct  the
Municipal Accountant and the HRMO to:

   1.   ensure  that  the  net  take  home  pay  of  all  employees  of  the
      Municipality of Culaba is not below the minimum amount  prescribed  by
      law;


   2. verify  the  paying  capacity  of  an  employee  before  signing  loan
      applications so that the mandated net take home pay  of  the  employee
      will be complied;


   3. authorize deductions only if the loan  was  obtained  from  accredited
      financing institutions enumerated by law.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and  the   Municipal
Accountant submit immediately the lacking  disbursement  vouchers  with  the
supporting documents to avoid  penal  sanctions.    In  future,  submit  all
documents  on  the  transactions  of  the  LGU,  trial  balances  and  other
financial reports within the timeline prescribed.


      Further, we recommended that the  Local  Chief  Executive  impose  the
prescribed  penal  provisions  when  the  said  officials  fail  to   render
accounts.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the  BAC  and  the
designated Supply Officer to submit consistently to the Audit  Team,  within
five (5) days upon perfection, the Approved Contracts and  Purchase  Orders,
together  with  the  required  supporting  documents   to   preclude   audit
suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions.


      We further recommended that each project  documents  be  filed  in  an
orderly manner, contained individually in an  expanding  envelope,  properly
labeled according  to  the  Name  of  the  Project,  Project  ID,  Location,
Contractor, Contract Amount, Source of Fund and Date of Bidding.


      We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant process payments  to
contractors  and  suppliers  only  when  the  same  is  supported  with  the
Transmittal Letter of the BAC  Secretariat  that  the  complete  procurement
documents were already submitted and duly received by Audit Team.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  the   officials
concerned to submit all required  reports  to  the  Audit  Team  within  the
prescribed timelines for prompt evaluation of the agency’s  accomplishments,
timely communication of results and avoid the imposition  of  sanctions  for
non-rendition of reports and accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive  ensure  that  the  SWM
Focal Person implement fully the identified Plans, Programs  and  Activities
in the Solid Waste Management Annual Plan for an  efficient  utilization  of
the budget for the said program.   Also,  oversee  the  preparation  of  the
aforementioned Plan so that the targets embodied  therein  are  those  which
are actually and presently needed by the LGU and its constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and all the  members  of
the Local School  Board  ensure  that  programs  and  activities  for  Early
Childhood  Care  Development  are  incorporated  in  the  budget  for   SEF.
Henceforth,  discontinue  the  use  of  SEF  for  expenses  which  are   not
authorized to be funded therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

     1) constitute the Local Council for the Protection of Children  in  the
        LGU from among its department heads, with himself as the Chairman;
     2)  require  the  created  LCPC  to  identify  programs,  projects  and
        activities for child protection  and  welfare  and  prevention  of
        juvenile delinquency;
     3)  prepare  the  LCPC  Annual  Work  and  Financial  Plan  prioritized
        according to needs for integration in the  local  development  plan,
        and;
     4) ensure that funds for LCPC- is equivalent to 1% of the  IRA  of  the
        Municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory  Committee
to submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and  Equipment
on the completed physical inventory  to  the  Municipal  Accountant.   Also,
mandate the Municipal Accountant to reconcile  the  RPCPPE  with  accounting
records and investigate discrepancies noted.


      We also reiterate  our  prior  year’s  recommendations  requiring  the
Inventory Committee to conduct the physical inventory  of  PPE  every  year,
and submit a report on the results thereon.  We further recommend  that  the
AREs of such properties be renewed every three (3) years.

We recommend  that  the  Municipal  Mayor  require  each  program  and
project  coordinator  to  fast-track  the  implementation  of  programs  and
projects funded by the National Government in accordance with  the  timeline
provided for in the MOA and require them to submit the  Liquidation  Reports
and other documents to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommend that the LDRRMO prepare annually the LDRRMFIP before  the
start of the year to support the amount and the activities indicated in  the
AIP and ensure that the disbursements are authorized by  the  LDRRM  Council
and the Sangguniang Bayan.

      Also, we recommend that the LDRRMO submit the Report  on  Sources  and
Utilization of LDRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each  month
through the LDRRMC to the COA to immediately determine  if  the  utilization
is responsive to risk reduction.

We recommend that the Local School Board utilize the SEF for
maintenance of public schools or repair of school buildings, facilities  and
equipment by ensuring that such would be allocated  correspondingly  in  the
preparation of the annual Local School Board budget.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor ensure that only projects  which are
capital expenditures should be funded from the 20% development  fund  in
order  to  maximize  the  utilization  of  the  fund   for   socio-economic,
development and environmental outcome for its constituents.

      We also recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  maintain  Subsidiary
Ledgers for the 20% development fund to account for  all  disbursements  and
balances of the  fund  and  reconcile  immediately  the  balances  from  the
records of the Budget Officer.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant request from the Provincial
Government of Biliran the required quarterly accomplishment  report.   Also,
require the Municipal RYF Council to  prepare  the  updated  6-year  updated
strategic plan in order that  the objective/vision,  mission  and major  final
output of this program was fully  attained.

that the BAC    
should ensure   
that the        
Abstracts of    
Canvass or Price
Quotation be    
attached on     
procurements    
using the       
alternative mode
of shopping or  
negotiated      
procurement as  
an evidence that
they have       
evaluated the   
submitted       
canvass price or
price quotation.
Also, we        
recommend that  
the Municipal   
Accountant      
should be       
vigilant in     
reviewing the   
supporting      
documents       
attached to a   
claim before    
processing it.

We recommend    
that the BAC,   
through its BAC 
Secretariat     
submit the      
required        
documents as    
mandated by COA
Regulations. We 
also recommend  
that the        
Municipal Mayor 
ensures the     
immediate       
submission of   
the said        
documents.

We recommend    
that he         
Municipal Mayor,
together with   
its GAD Focal   
Point System,   
should oversee  
and coordinate   
with the DILG   
Regional Office 
in order to      
facilitate the  
approval of the
its Annual GPBs.
Further, we     
recommend that  
the local       
sangguniang     
should first    
obtain the      
approved GPB    
before          
incorporating it
and enacting the
Annual Budget of
the municipality.

We recommend    
that the        
Municipal Mayor 
should ensure   
timely          
submission of   
the LGU’s GPB   
and GAD AR to   
the Audit Team  
as required by  
COA regulations.

We recommend    
that Management 
should granting 
cash advances to
an employee if  
the previous    
ones are not yet
liquidated. We  
also recommend  
that the        
Municipal       
Accountant      
should attach a 
certification   
that previous   
cash advances of
the employee    
were already    
liquidated as   
part of the     
supporting      
documents for   
purpose of      
granting cash   
advances.

We recommend    
that the six (6)
government      
officials       
involved        
immediately     
refund in the   
full the amount
of travelling   
expenses and    
registration    
expenses claimed
on the said     
travel.

We recommend    
that Management 
should strictly 
adhere to the   
requirements of 
relevant rules  
with regard to  
the timely      
preparation and
submission of    
monthly bank    
reconciliation  
statements. We  
also recommend  
that the        
Municipal       
Accountant      
should write a  
letter to the   
concerned bank 
demanding such  
bank statements.
